title: All on-chain state and code is public
description: Anyone can read every storage slot and decompile bytecode, so 'private' Solidity visibility hides nothing from observers.
body: Use when deciding what to store or how to handle secrets on-chain. Solidity's private/internal visibility only restricts contract-to-contract access; the actual storage and bytecode are fully readable by anyone running a node, and pending transactions are visible in the mempool before they confirm. Do not store secrets, keys, or sensitive data on-chain, and do not assume an unrevealed value (commit) is hidden once revealed in calldata. Actions: keep secrets off-chain, use commit-reveal with hashes for fairness, and assume the mempool is watched. Validation signals include a review confirming no sensitive data in storage or calldata. Risk boundary: treating on-chain storage as private leaks the data permanently; this is a critical and irreversible disclosure.
